text.title: AR Design Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying ar-design-patterns; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Objects placed by camera position drift as the user walks; users see content sliding off shelves.
antipattern.root-cause: Designer assumed world-lock = anchored; runtime treats world-lock as relative to initial camera pose.
antipattern.fix: Anchor to a plane or saved anchor; on track-loss, relocalise against the saved anchor.

antipattern.symptom: UI overlay drawn in front of a real person occluding it; depth perception breaks.
antipattern.root-cause: Occlusion not enabled or UI drawn in screen space rather than world space.
antipattern.fix: Enable people occlusion + scene depth; place UI in world space where appropriate.

antipattern.symptom: Tracking lost; UI freezes on stale frame; user thinks app crashed.
antipattern.root-cause: App treats track loss as recoverable without surfacing it.
antipattern.fix: Show reset prompt with 'Move camera slowly' guidance; preserve anchor list.
